explanations.1: This imports tools from Dagger, a framework that lets you build containerized pipelines—think of it like Docker but with Python code instead of shell scripts.
explanations.2: This imports Annotated and typing helpers, which let you add extra information to function parameters (like default values) without changing how Python sees them.
explanations.4: The @object_type decorator tells Dagger to treat this class as a reusable component in your pipeline that can be called from other tools.
explanations.7: The Annotated syntax with DefaultPath means this Directory parameter will automatically use the 'src' folder if the caller doesn't specify one—it's a smart default.
explanations.11: The \033[33m and \033[0m are ANSI color codes that make the text yellow in your terminal output; it's just for readability when the pipeline runs.
explanations.13: This starts a chain of operations (called a fluent interface) where each line modifies the container step-by-step, and the entire thing is awaited because it's async.
explanations.20: .stdout() captures and returns the text output from the pylint command instead of just running it silently.
